transcript.text: Hey, there.
It's on mute.
Hello.
Hi.
How are you doing?
I'm good.
How are you?
I'm pretty good.
I'm going to be on.
I'm trying to squeeze in my lunch.
It's a hectic meeting day, and it just arrived, so I'm going to be off camera for a little bit, but I'll be on.
Yeah, happens to us a lot.
Don't worry about it.
And, Erik, I hope you saw the – sorry for moving the schedule of our product walkthrough so much.
Our SE, who's going to lead it, had a couple of customer calls that happened last minute, so I shipped it to 5.15 p.m.
East Coast time, so I think 2.15 your time?
I'm Midwest, so still before 5.
Okay, awesome.
Yeah, sorry for the late call, but it was the only time to get it done, and I'd rather have it sooner than later.
Yeah, no worries.
I've actually got a call at, like, 6 o'clock tonight with a different client, so – Okay.
They're the ones making me stay up late.
Duly noted.
I'm glad I'm on your good side.
Are they on the West Coast?
Yeah.
Okay.
All right.
All right.
Well, I think we can get started.
I've added you to the agenda, so you should get it in your email.
It should be an email from Notion.
So, yeah.
So, updates from last week.
We did get feedback from the team on the calibration blog that we delivered.
Okay.
We've gone through and implemented feedback.
We've kind of, like, responded to every comment.
We haven't resolved it yet.
We haven't resolved any comments yet.
But just in case anybody else wants to, kind of, provide feedback.
But let us know if this is it.
Like, if anybody else is supposed to provide feedback.
I think that's it.
I know I tagged in Sev.
I think in the chat yesterday, in the Slack channel yesterday.
But if he hasn't got feedback, I don't want to hold up the process any further.
He could probably be part of the review process moving forward.
Okay, okay, that makes sense.
All right, I think then we can just close out the feedback, and then we're going to move, like, change the status in Airtable to just ready to publish.
And then we can, yeah, like, move forward with staging that blog in your CMS.
And then we are working on three additional blogs at the moment.
So we're working on lateral movement, lapse exploit, and how to detect golden ticket attacks.
So we are hoping to deliver those, like, early next week.
And then we can kind of start, like, scaling.
We want to get to five per week.
And I think, like, doing three kind of helps us, like, get enough feedback without, like, overwhelming you with, like, five blogs at a time.
Okay.
Yeah.
Okay.
And then I think we can also set up, just to make these reviews easier, we can set up an automation in Airtable.
It will just send emails to a specific, like, list of people every time an article is labeled as, like, reviewing.
So I don't know, like, who should I add to that list?
Like, probably Sev.
Yeah, Sev, Kirsten, and Jason will probably be the best.
Yep.
And then I wouldn't necessarily rely on all three of them to review, but if you could get two out of the three, that would be, that would be good.
I know it's, depending on the week, things, people are pretty slammed, so I don't want to hold up the process too much.
Okay.
And then I'll try to review as much as well.
Probably not to the layer of technical expertise, but just kind of looking at like overall kind of brand tone and feel.
Yeah, yeah.
Sounds good.
Okay, perfect.
We can do that moving forward just to make things easier.
And then you have approved like 16 topics to get us started.
So we have like a backlog of 13 for the next two weeks, which is pretty good.
I know Kirsten did flag that there could be some potentially duplicate topics.
I'm going to go through and kind of double check because the keywords should all be like unique.
We do try to provide keywords that are related to each other, but we try to stay away from, try to verify that like there aren't any duplicates.
The titles could be sometimes like similar, however, the titles that are provided.
Like in the content OS, they are just like draft titles.
So when we actually create these blog ideas, we upload a list of target keywords that we manually research.
And then we use our article generation, our assignment generation pipeline, which is connected to a SEMrush API.
And the API gives us information about the top ranking content.
And then the AI basically generates a title that could meet the search intent and that is quite similar to the top ranking content.
And it's gotten better.
Like I distinctly remember because I've been at GrowthX for almost a year.
Like a year ago, the titles were just, I had to rewrite them every single time.
These days, the titles are a lot better right off the bat.
But we still do change them because we change the structure.
We might like add something or remove something and we want to like make sure.
But the article, like the title is accurate.
So just wanted to flag that.
Okay.
Thank you.
Okay.
So I'll go through that and just like make sure that there's nothing in here that's like potentially duplicative.
And then for, okay, so we are working on three blogs and we have set up an image generation pipeline for the blog posts that we're working on.
So we kind of used the existing imagery that you have on your blog.
And this is like what it looks like right now.
So I can actually like expand a couple so you can see better.
Okay.
I like these.
Yeah, they don't seem like they're not.
I think when we say like images are AI generated, I think sometimes people get a little bit hesitant.
But yeah, they're like six fingers or.
Exactly.
Yeah.
No, no, no.
We tried to stick to graphics because the AI just does a lot better at graphics and illustrations than human beings.
That makes sense.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I think like we get, so per article, like it's going to generate like at least like five or 10 different options.
So we try to pick the best option out of the bunch.
And we also like upload that to your CMS.
So, you know, you don't have to like provide images of your own.
Okay, cool.
I think if this looks good to you, I mean, I can also share these examples in Slack and then the rest of the team can weigh in.

If you have like a designer on your team, they can also kind of share some feedback because these images are generated using a pretty detailed prompt that our designer Katja creates and then it's upload.

And then GPT actually creates these images, so we can always like adjust, for example, if like the castles, if they look a little too playful, like a little too childish, we can like add an instruction to make sure the imagery is like professional, doesn't include any like playful elements.
Yeah, honestly, think, now that you bring it to our attention, if any of those images were to probably get any scrutiny, would probably be the castles.
Yeah.
So, but please run that by Rick and Kirsten, and then maybe Bryce.
Bryce probably will have a strong opinion about the images as well.
Okay, okay.
But I do like the other ones.
Okay, okay, yeah, that's helpful.
I can share them in a Google Drive.
Okay, perfect.
And then did I ever, okay, this is our test, but.
Um, did I ever, um, show you how the article generation process looks like?
No, I have not.
No, okay, I can actually do a, like, quick walkthrough, um, because we do have, like, the Argentic pipeline, um, set up right now.
So, um, this is Atlas, this is our, like, content generation platform that we built in-house.

Um, we've had it for about, I think, three to four months, um, and we're kind of constantly improving it as we get new clients, new cases, new use cases emerge, and we're constantly, like, just trying to improve it.
Um, so we basically have, like, two article generation pipelines set up.
One is the Argentic one, and then the other one is the standard one.
So far, we've been, um, using the standard one for a lot of our clients.
Um, but where it falls short, or where we had to improve.
It wasn't the research step.
So up until now, we've been using perplexity to do the research.
So it's been like the process is divided into like multiple steps.
So the input is like a keyword.
We give it like it could be anything.
It could be like a golden ticket attack, for example.
So, sorry, I have to hide our faces.
And then, you know, we kind of like click through all of these steps.
And at first, it will generate like a draft, like a brief.
And it connects to a Sermarsh API.
It researches the top ranking content.
And it gives us suggestions on like the keywords that we should include.
What are like the sections that the article should cover.
But typically, we will also heavily like audit this step.
So,
And, like, we might completely change the outline, depending on our, like, using our own manual research.

And then we will run the deep research step, which takes the outline, and then it uses perplexity to find all the information the AI needs to actually write an article that's in-depth and isn't shallow.
And then it generates an outline that we review, and then the draft, we review the draft, and then we, you know, deliver it to you.
But the Argentic Pipeline, and that's the one that we've actually been using for you, it does the research step differently.
So let me just actually go right here.
So we give it a keyword, and then it uses a different researcher called
So it's a more advanced research tool than perplexity, and it can find information, it can find a lot more information than perplexity, basically.
But what's really interesting about this step is that it will research a specific topic, and then it will ask itself questions about the answers.
So it's grading its output at the same time.
So, for example, let me actually inspect so you can actually see what the process looks like.
So, for example, we gave it these instructions, like we're writing an article to cover this topic, this is the direction for the article, so we give it like a very kind of specific instructions.
And then it generates a research plan.
So then it gives, like, it generates a research objective.
So it identified that.

It's supposed to create a comprehensive technical guide explaining the differences between zero trust and least privilege, and then it's going to generate questions that it assumes it needs the answer to provide information about this topic.
And then it collects the answers, and then it evaluates the research quality.
So, for example, for the first question, the score is 0.8b8.
meets criteria, but it also identified gaps.

So, it realized that it was missing, like, specific cost breakdowns, that it could benefit from more detailed vendor integration specifics beyond Microsoft, and it also, like, had limited discussion of change management and stakeholder buy-in strategies.
And then, like, it's going to go back, and if it did not pass, it's going to go back and try to find more information to actually plug those research gaps.
So, it's basically generating questions for the reason.
search, then it collects the answers and then it grades those answers and tries to figure out, okay, what are the other questions that I could ask?
And our fact checker, sorry.
So our fact checker step is also like more advanced, and it's better than in the regular pipeline that we had.
So it's really able to catch any statements that are inaccurate or just cannot be verified.

And if a statement cannot be verified, it's going to change the paragraph to say like, while industry practitioners recommend this course of action, there is no documentation supporting the claim that X is going to result in Y.
And then we can spot that and be like, okay, like we can remove the section or we decide what we're going to do with it.
So it just allows us to kind of, you know, kind of be more, have more confidence in the output.
Okay.
It's pretty cool.
Yeah.
Yeah.
But it basically like
Runs like from the research and then it kind of adds internal links and add the, adds the, generates the draft.
So yeah, from like end to the end process, like it's very controlled by us.
So it's, I do like always like to show it to the people that we work with because you never really like know what we do behind the scenes.
And now when you see like where we work, I think it gives you like a better idea of how we generate content.
And if you have any ideas for the content that you want to create on the website, you know, you're always like welcome to share and, you know, you know how our process works.
So I think it might be even easier to kind of come up with ideas.
Yeah, this is very helpful.
Thank you.
Yeah, cool.
All right.
I'm going to get out of here because I think someone's generating, so I don't want to mess anything up.
Okay, cool.
And then what else is what else?
What else?
So, we did share the images.
I need to just check to see if the Looker dashboard is working properly.
Let me just take a look right now.
Let me see.
Okay.
Might not be validated yet.
I'll double check.
We just need to be granted access again.
Yeah, think he tried to do it this morning.
He said that he approved.
Know if you don't see any changes this afternoon and I'll follow up with them and provide me whatever instruction you think is needed to get it done.
Okay.
Okay.
Cool.
All right.
Perfect.
Great.
So, yeah, next steps.
We're going to resolve the comments on the calibration blog.
We're going to...
Draft it in your CMS.
We're not going to publish.
We're just going to leave it there.
And then you can take a look and see if it looks good and you can push it live.
And we're also going to be in touch soon with the new blogs.
Okay, perfect.
Thank you.
Cool, cool.
Do you have any other questions?
Anything else you wanted to cover today?
Nope, not on my end.
Thank you.
And then I'll see you this afternoon or this evening for me, Erik.
Yep, absolutely.
Okay, cool.
Thank you so much.
All right, thank you.
Bye-bye.
See you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on blog content progress, review processes, and production pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=73.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [The "calibration" blog is approved for staging; GrowthX will implement feedback and prepare it for SpecterOps to publish.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=85.0)
  - [GrowthX is drafting three new blogs ("lateral movement," "laps exploit," "golden ticket attacks") to test the review process before scaling to five per week.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=158.0)
  - [A new Airtable automation will notify reviewers (Sev, Kirsten, Jason) when an article is ready, requiring feedback from only two of the three to prevent bottlenecks.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)
  - [GrowthX demoed its "Argentic" content pipeline, which uses a self-correcting research agent and advanced fact-checking to ensure technical accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=535.0)

## Topics

### Blog Content & Review Process

  - [**"Calibration" Blog:**](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=85.0)
      - [Feedback implemented; comments will remain open for potential final input from Sev.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=85.0)
      - [Status in Airtable → "ready to publish."](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=137.0)
      - [Next: Staging in SpecterOps' CMS for final review before publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=137.0)
  - [**New Blogs in Progress:**](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=158.0)
      - ["Lateral movement"](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=158.0)
      - ["Laps exploit"](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=158.0)
      - ["How to detect golden ticket attacks"](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=158.0)
      - [Delivery target: early next week.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=158.0)
  - [**Review Workflow Automation:**](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)
      - [An Airtable automation will email reviewers when an article's status is "reviewing."](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)
      - [**Reviewers:** Sev, Kirsten, Jason.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=213.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Requires feedback from only two of the three to prevent bottlenecks from busy schedules.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=213.0)
      - [Meshach will also review for brand tone.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=240.0)
  - [**Topic Backlog:**](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=257.0)
      - [16 approved topics provide a 2-week backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=257.0)
      - [Kirsten flagged potential duplicates; GrowthX will audit the list.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=257.0)
      - [**Context:** Draft titles are AI-generated based on SEMrush data; final titles are manually refined to ensure uniqueness.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=267.0)

### AI-Generated Images

  - [GrowthX's new pipeline generates graphics and illustrations, which perform better than AI-generated photos.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)
  - [**Feedback:** The "castle" images may appear too playful and require refinement for a professional tone.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=459.0)
  - [**Next:** GrowthX will share examples in Slack for feedback from Rick, Kirsten, and Bryce.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=459.0)

### Content Generation Pipeline Demo

  - [Aida demoed the "Argentic" pipeline in the Atlas platform, designed for technical accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=535.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=750.0)
    1.  [**Research Plan:** Generates an objective and questions for a topic (e.g., "Zero Trust vs. Least Privilege").](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=750.0)
    2.  [**Self-Correction:** Collects answers, then evaluates them for gaps (e.g., missing cost breakdowns, vendor specifics).](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
    3.  [**Iteration:** If quality is low, the agent asks new questions to fill gaps, repeating until the research passes.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=828.0)
    4.  [**Advanced Fact-Checking:** Verifies statements and flags unverified claims, allowing manual review.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=848.0)
  - [**Benefit:** This process provides high confidence in the technical accuracy of the final draft.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=848.0)

### Admin & Logistics

  - [**Product Walkthrough:** Rescheduled to 5:15 PM ET today (2:15 PM PT) due to a last-minute customer call for the SpecterOps SE.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=29.0)
  - [**Looker Dashboard:** Access is pending. Meshach will follow up with the team if the issue is not resolved today.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=964.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=1014.0)
      - [Resolve comments on the "calibration" blog and stage it in the CMS.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=1014.0)
      - [Deliver three new blogs ("lateral movement," "laps exploit," "golden ticket attacks") early next week.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=158.0)
      - [Set up the Airtable review notification automation for Sev, Kirsten, and Jason.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)
      - [Audit the topic backlog for potential duplicates.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=257.0)
      - [Share AI-generated image examples in Slack for team feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=459.0)
  - [**Meshach:**](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=964.0)
      - [Follow up on Looker dashboard access if not resolved today.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=964.0)
  - [**Erik & Meshach:**](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=29.0)
      - [Attend the product walkthrough at 5:15 PM ET today.](https://fathom.video/share/2xe6hon5pCAn37eGpHhAUFr_ysnczY_v?tab=summary&timestamp=29.0)

action_items.description: Close Notion comments on Calibration blog; set Airtable Ready to publish; stage in CMS
Set up Airtable automation to email Sev, Kirsten, Jason on Reviewing; require 2/3
Review 16-topic backlog for duplicates; verify unique keywords
Share AI image examples in Drive; post Slack link for Rick, Kirsten, Bryce
Verify Looker dashboard access; if not working, Meshach follow up w/ Looker team
